No draws, poor postflop playability. Deep stacks, but folding trash preserves chips for better spots. Tight preflop strategy to build stack long-term in tournament.

With the button (Elon) folded this is heads-up against the big blind. A2o is a very strong open-shove/raise candidate heads-up — it has high showdown value and blocks some of BB’s stronger Axs hands. I want to take the initiative, isolate the BB, and pick up the blind or see a flop with position (in heads-up the small blind acts last postflop). A standard 3x BB open is an efficient size: gives fold equity, keeps the pot manageable, and balances value and protection. Plan: c-bet many flops and play for pot control; fold to a large 3-bet since A2o is vulnerable, call smaller 3-bets and play postflop using position. Total to commit (including my 50 SB) = 300.

I am in the Big Blind with 2s 4d, which is an extremely weak hand. Minni, who has been very active and currently holds a large chip lead, raised to 300 from the Small Blind. Although I only need to call 200 more to see a flop, 2s 4d has very poor playability and I will be out of position for the rest of the hand. It is better to preserve my stack and wait for a more favorable spot.

In a 3-handed game the button should open wide. 9♦8♠ is a playable connector in position with ~212bb effective stacks, so I want to isolate the blinds and build the pot with positional advantage. A standard 3x open to 300 fits the table tendencies and gives good fold equity. If I get a cold 3-bet I'll usually fold 98o (or reassess vs a small sizing/loose opponent); if called I'll play aggressively in position — c-bet favorable flops, float and leverage implied odds to realize straights/second-best hands.

I am in the Small Blind with Jc3c. The Button (Minni) has raised to 300. J3 offsuit is a very weak hand to play out of position against a loose-aggressive opponent like Minni, who has been active in many recent hands and has a significant chip lead. Calling 250 more to play a 450 pot with a weak hand and being out of position for the rest of the hand is a negative expected value play. Folding is the most prudent decision here to preserve my stack.

Facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds, pot is 450, and I need to call 200 more from the BB with T8d suited. This gives excellent pot odds of about 2.25:1 (31% equity needed). T8s is a strong speculative hand with flush and straight potential, good playability postflop despite being OOP. Button ranges are wide in this spot (likely 30-40% of hands), and T8s has solid equity (~35-40%) against that range. Minni has been aggressive with frequent button raises and steals, but calling keeps the pot multi-street friendly with my suited connector. Folding is too tight given odds and hand strength; 3-betting is unnecessary as the hand prefers set-mining/flush-draw realization over polarization. Deep stacks (90BB) support calling to realize equity.

Flop board is 2d-2c-Ks, a paired, king-high board that hits villain's wide button raising range frequently (Kx, Ax, broadway). My Td8d has no pair, no strong draw (only three diamonds total for weak backdoor flush draw, no straight draw), and ten-high has zero showdown value. OOP first to act, deep stacks (90BB+), pot 650. Checking is mandatory to pot control and realize equity only if villain checks behind (possible on paired board). Donk betting is a bluff with poor blockers and weak range advantage here; villain likely bets most of her continuing range. Plan to check-fold to any bet of reasonable size, preserving stack for better spots. This aligns with GTO in positionally disadvantaged speculative hand realization gone dry.

I opened wide with 9♦8♠ on the button and Elon (BB) just called, so my flop strategy should be aggressive. The flop 2♦-2♣-K♠ is very dry and misses most of my range, but it’s an excellent continuation-bet spot — I can credibly represent Kx or a broad range of overpairs and pick off lots of unpaired hands and small pairs that defended the BB. Elon has shown a tendency to check/call a lot, so I expect many of his hands to fold to pressure, while the Kx hands will often merely call. I’ll bet for fold equity but keep the pot manageable since I have no real equity and Elon is deep enough to call. Bet ~1/2 pot (325): large enough to get value/folds from the right part of his range, but not so large that I risk a huge portion of my stack with a pure bluff. If he folds, I pick up the pot; if he calls I will re-evaluate on the turn and give up to significant aggression unless the turn meaningfully improves my story.

Facing a bet of 325 into 650 on a 2d2cKs flop (pot now 975), with Td8d OOP. My hand has no pair, no straight draw, only a weak backdoor diamond flush draw, and ten-high showdown value is negligible. Minni's button range hits this board hard (Kx, Ax, broadway, overpairs), and her aggression continues postflop as expected from history of frequent steals and c-bets. Pot odds require ~25% equity, but I have far less (~10-15%) against her continuing range. Calling commits to multi-street OOP with air on a paired king-high board, turning dead money into losses. Check-folding preserves stack for better spots in deep-stack tournament play. No value in raising as a bluff without strong blockers or range advantage.

I am on the button with T2s. While the suitedness and the Ten offer some playability, this hand is generally too weak to open raise from the button in a 3-handed game, especially given the aggressive play observed from Minni in previous hands. Folding is the standard and most disciplined play here to preserve my stack.

Pocket 5s is a strong hand in SB vs BB heads-up after Button folds. It has good equity (~50-55% vs a typical BB defending range), set-mining potential, and blocks low pairs. Raising builds the pot for value against worse pairs, broadways, and suited connectors that BB defends wide. Minni's history shows she calls raises frequently with speculative hands. A raise to 3x BB (300 total) is standard sizing from SB: denies equity to draws, gets value, and applies pressure given effective stacks (~87BB). Limping risks multiway pots or giving free flops to overcards; folding is too tight. Pot odds post-raise justify calling stations staying in.

I have 9♠8♣ in the big blind facing Elon’s small‑blind open to 300. The pot is 400 and it costs me only 200 more to call (300 total committed). 9‑8 off is a playable, connected hand with decent flop equity and good implied odds versus what is likely a fairly wide SB opening range. 3‑betting OOP here bloats the pot with a marginal hand and risks a 4‑bet; folding is too passive given the cheap price. I’ll call to see a flop and play based on texture — raise or shove only if I hit a strong draw or pair plus good equity.

Pocket 5s is an underpair on an Ace-high flop with two hearts (Ah Jd 7h), which heavily favors Minni's BB calling range including Ax, Jx, suited connectors, and flush draws. My hand has limited showdown value and no strong draws (one heart doesn't give a flush draw). Equity is around 30-40% against her range. As first to act heads-up, betting thin for value risks folding out worse hands while vulnerable to raises from better hands or draws. Checking accomplishes pot control, allows realization of equity through showdown if she checks behind, induces bluffs from missed draws, and keeps the option to check-raise strong turns or fold to heavy aggression. Minni's history shows passive tendencies post-flop (frequent check-calls), making check optimal to extract value without bloating the pot prematurely. Stacks are deep (~85BB effective), supporting check-fold/raise dynamics.

I have 9♠8♣ on A♥ J♦ 7♥ — no pair and only modest equity. I have a gutshot to a 10 (4 outs) plus 3 nines + 3 eights (6 outs) for ~10 outs total (~38% to improve by river), but most pair-outs will still be behind common caller hands (A‑x, J‑x, medium pairs). Elon was the preflop raiser and checked the flop, so his range contains many Ax/Jx and some pairs — a small bet here would be a bluff that will often be called. With deep stacks and a marginal hand, I don’t want to inflate the pot; better to control the pot and realize equity for free. Plan: check and see the turn; call small/medium bets to look for improvement (10 / pair of 9/8) but fold to large aggression unless I improve, and if I do hit I’ll start betting or raising for value.

Pocket 5s remains an underpair on a paired Ace-high board (Ah Jd 7h As), facing Minni's wide BB defending range post my pre-flop raise. The turn As improves her Ax hands to two-pair/top pair, kills heart flush draws, and reduces my equity further (~20-30% vs her range including AA, AK, AJ, pocket pairs below Aces). No strong draws or showdown value premium in my hand. As first to act heads-up with pot control priority on deep stacks (~85BB effective), checking induces bluffs from her missed draws/suited connectors, realizes equity if she checks behind (her passive history: frequent check-calls, few post-flop leads), and avoids value-owning ourselves with thin bets that get raised by better. Betting risks folding out worse (e.g., Jx, low pairs) while vulnerable to raises from monsters; check sets up fold/call/raise dynamics profitably long-term. Consistent with flop check strategy.

I check to control the pot and see the river. The board A♥ J♦ 7♥ A♠ heavily favors many hands in Elon's SB raising range (Ax, AJ, some two-pairs or slowplayed sets). Elon has checked both flop and turn, which looks like pot‑control with a showdown hand or weakness — not a spot to bluff large. I hold 9♠8♣ with no pair and about 10 outs (three 9s, three 8s, four 10s) — roughly 21–22% to improve on the river. With deep stacks and a marginal draw, I don’t want to bloat the pot by betting or turning this into a bluff; best line is pot control and try to realize equity for free. Plan: check now; on the river call small-to-medium bets (as a rule of thumb a bet up to ≈230 chips is defensible given ~21–22% equity vs the current 600 pot), fold to large-sized aggression unless I’ve improved, and if I hit a 9/8/10 on the river I’ll pursue value (bet or raise as appropriate).

Pocket 5s arrives at the river on a paired Ace-high board with a completed heart flush (Ah Jd 7h As 2h). The board heavily favors Minni's BB calling range post my preflop raise: trips+ with any Ax, AJ two-pair, flushes (three hearts on board), sets, straights (T8 possible but narrow), and Jx/low pairs. My underpair has ~15-25% equity against her range, decent showdown value vs her bluffs/missed draws (88-TT, KQ suited, connectors that whiffed), but vulnerable to value. Checks through flop/turn suggest she lacks strong made hands/draws initially, but river flush completion improves her holdings. As first to act heads-up (~14BB pot, 85BB+ effective stacks), checking prioritizes pot control: induces bluffs from Jx/low pairs fearing flush, realizes equity via showdown if she checks behind (fits her passive check-call history), and allows profitable calls to small value bets (e.g., AJ/Jx) or folds to large ones (nuts). Betting thin risks folding out worse while getting raised/floated by better (Ax, flush); pot odds poor for hero calls post-bet. Consistent with prior pot-control strategy, check maximizes EV long-term vs Minni's tendencies (frequent passive lines, occasional river leads).

Check. On the river I have 9♠8♣ on A♥ J♦ 7♥ A♠ 2♥ — no pair, no backdoor. Elon was the SB raiser preflop but checked flop and turn, which looks like pot‑control with an A‑x or a showdown candidate rather than a missed hand looking to bluff. A river bluff here would be thin and is unlikely to fold enough of Elon’s range to justify risking chips. The pot is only 600 and my stack is large, so preserving chips and taking the showdown is the prudent line. If Elon fires a small bet I’ll reassess (call only versus a believable frequency of bluffs; fold to large sizing), but with action checked down I’ll check and show down.

3-handed on the button facing the blinds with 97o, a playable hand with straight draw potential and decent high card value. In position, button opening range is very wide (50%+ of hands), and 97o fits comfortably. Opponents' histories show they fold frequently preflop (e.g., Flash and Minni often fold to button opens). Raising steals blinds ~40-50% of the time and puts me in position postflop. Standard sizing 3x BB (300 total) balances value and bluffs effectively. Stacks are deep enough for postflop play if called.

Elon (button) opened to 300. I’m SB with 8♠7♥ — a marginal but playable, connected hand. The raise size is small (3x) so calling is cheap (250 more) given the 450 pot and my healthy stack. I don’t want to 3-bet out of position with this hand, and folding is too passive vs what looks like a wide button open. I’ll call to see a flop, realize equity, and play cautiously OOP: give up to large aggression on dry boards, continue with two-pairs/strong draws, and probe/raise only with clear equity or strong hands.

I am holding King-Jack suited in the Big Blind, which is a premium hand in a 3-handed game. With the Button (Elon) raising and the Small Blind (Minni) flatting, this is an ideal spot for a squeeze play. By raising to 1200, I exert pressure on Elon's wide button opening range and Minni's likely capped calling range. This allows me to take control of the hand and potentially win the pot pre-flop. If called, KJs has excellent post-flop playability and equity, even while playing out of position.

Facing a large 3-bet (to 1200, 4x my open) from Flash in the BB after Minni called my button raise with 9s7c offsuit. This hand is in my wide button opening range 3-handed but has very poor equity against a strong 3-bet range (likely JJ+, AK, AQ+). No suited/connectors for multiway potential, dominated and reverse dominated often. Calling 900 into 2700 pot gets ~3:1 but OOP postflop with bad hand is -EV long-term. Raising has no fold equity against this sizing and commits too much stack (~21% effective). History shows Flash 3-bets aggressively but this spot screams strength after SB call. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I originally planned to defend the 3x button open in the small blind with 8♠7♥, but Flash (BB) 3-bet to 1,200. The pot is 1,800 and I must call 900 more to see a flop — that requires roughly ~25% equity to be profitable immediately. My hand is an unsuited, marginal connector and I'll be out of position postflop against a likely value-heavy 3-bet range (TT+/AQ+ plus some strong broadways). A 4-bet bluff OOP with 8-7o is too loose and calling commits chips into a difficult spot where I’m often dominated and have limited fold equity. I’ll preserve my deep stack and fold here rather than pay the extra 900.

I have T♦Q♠ on the button in a three-handed pot — this is a hand I want to be raising with, not limping. Position plus the heads-up dynamic makes Q-T offsuit a profitable open: it picks up the blinds’ wide defending ranges and gives me good playability postflop. I’ll open to 300 (3x the big blind). This sizing buys fold equity, makes callers pay to see a flop, and keeps the pot manageable versus the 10k/8.7k stacks. Plan: if called I’ll use position to c‑bet favorable flops and realize equity; if faced with a large, committed 3‑bet I’ll fold TQ, but I’ll consider calling smaller 3‑bets to play postflop in position.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Five offsuit. The Button (Minni) has opened for 3x the big blind. A5o is a reasonable hand to defend in this position against a wide Button opening range. While 3-betting is a viable strategy to take the initiative, calling allows me to see a flop for a relatively low price with an Ace-high hand, though I will be playing out of position. Given the stack sizes and the tournament stage, I'll choose to call and see how the flop develops.

Preflop in BB facing button raise to 300 and SB call. Pot 700, calling 200 more (pot odds 3.5:1, need ~22% equity). 6d9h offsuit has decent playability with straight potential (open-ender possible), suited-ish feel but rainbow. Button range wide (steal-y), SB call range decent but not nuts. Equity likely sufficient multiway (~25%+ vs ranges). Deep stacks, early tournament; defend wide OOP vs button. Postflop plan: check/call/turn decisions based on board texture, avoid bloating pot OOP. Folding too tight, 3bet not ideal blocker/strength.

The flop of Kd 9s 4h does not connect well with my Ace-Five offsuit. I am out of position against both the pre-flop raiser (Minni) and the big blind (Elon). Since this board favors the button's opening range, a check is the most prudent move. I have some showdown value with Ace-high, but I cannot comfortably bet into two opponents on this texture. I will check and likely fold to any significant aggression.

Middle pair with 6 kicker on Kd-9s-4h flop in 3-way pot, OOP after SB check. Decent showdown value but vulnerable to better pairs (Kx, sets) and draws. Preflop calling range was wide in BB vs button open and SB call. Checking maintains pot control OOP, allows seeing button's action (likely c-bet wide). Can call small bets profitably or fold to aggression. Thin value bet risks getting raised off equity or isolating SB who may have worse; check better for pot control and multiway dynamics. Plan: check/call turn if board safe, reevaluate river.

I opened to 300 preflop with QTo and both blinds called, so the pot is 900. Flop Kd‑9s‑4h completely missed my hand — no pair and no meaningful draw — while this texture heavily favors the callers’ ranges (Ks, 9x, small pairs, many flats). Bluff‑betting into two players is thin and likely to be called; I’m in position and should control the pot and realize equity rather than force a bluff. Action: check and see a turn. If faced with a small stab (~<=1/3 pot) I can call to see a card; I’ll fold or seriously consider folding to large barrels/raises since I have little showdown value. If checked through I’ll reassess on the turn and look to bet only on favorable cards (Q/T/J or safe backdoors) or use a delayed bluff when the board and reads line up.

The turn Ten of Spades connects the board further, creating several potential straight draws and hitting middle-pair ranges. After the flop checked through, it's possible no one has a strong King, but Ace-high still has very low equity and no significant draws on this texture. I will check to see how the action develops; if there is any betting from either opponent, I intend to fold given the lack of hand strength and potential for being dominated.

On the turn, the board is Kd-9s-4h-Ts, giving me middle pair (9s) with a weak 6 kicker in a 3-way pot OOP. Flash checks again after the checked-around flop, signaling possible weakness, but the Ts completes straight draws (e.g., QJ, 78) and strengthens the spade flush draw. Minni checked back flop from button, likely weak or trapping, but betting OOP risks raising off my equity or getting called by better (Kx, straights). Pot control remains key with marginal showdown value; checking keeps the pot small multiway, induces bluffs on river if needed, and avoids bloating vs. deep stacks. Plan: check, evaluate river based on action/board/runout. Folding equity from bet low vs. calling ranges.

I opened to 300 preflop with QTo and both blinds called, so the pot is 900. The turn (T) pairs my Ten and gives me second pair (T with a Q kicker) on K‑9‑4‑T. This is a “good card” for my range — it can get value from worse tens, 9x and small pairs and charges two‑spade flush draws — while still being vulnerable to Kx and the rare JQ straight. In position I want to extract thin value and deny equity while keeping the pot manageable, so I’ll make a small 1/3‑pot bet of 300. If I face a large raise I will fold; if called I’ll re‑evaluate on the river.

On the turn, the board is Kd-9s-4h-Ts. My Ace-high (As 5h) has no significant equity and no real draws on this texture. Minni (Button) has placed a delayed continuation bet of 300 into a 900 pot after the flop checked through. While the bet size is small (1/3 pot), Ace-high is very unlikely to be the best hand here, especially with the 10 of spades connecting several straight draws and hitting middle-pair ranges. Additionally, Elon (Big Blind) is still to act behind me. There is no reason to continue with a hand that has such low showdown value and limited potential to improve. I will fold and conserve my stack.

Facing a small turn bet of 300 into a 1200 pot (~5:1 odds, needing ~17% equity). Middle pair (9s) with 6 kicker provides showdown value heads-up after Flash folds. Minni's button range (preflop raiser, flop check-back, small turn bet) likely includes draws (spade flush draw with Ts bringing third spade, QJ/J8 straight draws), weak Kx, Ax high, or bluffs probing weakness. Equity sufficient vs this wide range (~30-40% estimated). Calling preserves pot control OOP, realizes equity passively vs worse pairs/draws, avoids bloating pot with marginal strength. Raising commits too much without clear value; folding surrenders too much equity given price. Plan: call, check/call river selectively if board safe, fold to heavy aggression.

River heads-up OOP with middle pair 9s and 6 kicker on paired board (4h Kd 9s Ts 4c). Pot 1500, deep stacks. Minni's button range (PFR, flop check-back likely weak/draws, small turn bet after checks) includes missed spade draws (third spade on turn), gutshots (QJ, J8, etc.), weak Kx (KQ, KJ), Ax high, air/bluffs probing checked board. My 9s beats all misses, worse pairs (unlikely), but loses to Kx, 99, 44, KT, sets. Paired 4s helps some backdoor two-pair but doesn't complete major draws. First to act OOP prioritizes pot control with marginal showdown value; checking induces bluffs/value from worse, allows profitable calls to <50% pot bets (~30-40% equity vs range), extracts value passively without risking raise-off equity. Thin bet/raise commits too much unnecessarily vs calling range; folding discards equity. Long-term: disciplined OOP play preserves stack.

I have QTs — second pair (tens) with a decent kicker. I led the turn for 300 and Elon called, which is consistent with Kx, 9x, Tx, and small pairs/floaters. The river (4c) simply paired the board, creating trips for anyone holding a 4 but not removing many hands that already beat me (most notably Kx). Betting here would get called by better hands and will often fold worse hands that would pay me off; there are not many credible bluffs to push through. With a thin value window and real vulnerability to Kx, the lowest-variance +EV line is to check and take this to showdown. If Elon bets a small amount I’ll call for showdown; I’ll fold to a large jam that polarizes to stronger holdings.

I am on the button with Q2 suited. While 3-handed play requires a wider opening range, Q2s is at the very bottom of that range. Given that Minni (the Big Blind) has a significant chip lead and has shown a high level of aggression in previous hands, opening such a weak hand could put me in a difficult position post-flop or against a 3-bet. I will choose the disciplined path and fold this hand to maintain my stack for better opportunities.

Preflop from the small blind facing a big blind after button fold. Hand is 8s4h, offsuit gappers with no playability. Terrible hand equity, dominated by any calling range. Pot odds to complete are 150:50 (3:1) but postflop OOP against Minni who plays aggressively (frequent preflop raises and postflop aggression from history). Raising as a steal with this hand risks being called by better and playing OOP postflop in bad shape. Folding preserves stack early in tournament (hand 25/150, ~81BB deep) for better spots. Standard fold.

3-handed on the button after SB Minni folds her blind (dead money in pot) and BB Flash checks. Pot has 150 including dead 50 from SB. 4c8s is a marginal offsuit hand but in position vs a likely wide checking range from BB. Standard play is to raise wide (~45-50% hands) for value + fold equity. Min-raise to 200 applies pressure, risks little relative to stack (81BB deep), and takes initiative. BB folds often enough, or if calls, we have position and can c-bet flops well. Limping bad strategy (no fold equity, plays OOP postflop), folding too nitty missing dead money and position EV.

I’m Minni in the SB with A♦Q♥ facing a button open to 200 (pot 350, 150 to call). AQo is a strong hand in a 3-handed game — it plays well as a 3-bet for value and fold equity. Calling keeps me out of position and invites the BB to come along; a sized 3-bet both isolates the opener and applies pressure to marginal hands. I’ll raise to 900 total (≈4.5x the open). That size is big enough to discourage many speculative cold-calls from the BB and extract value/isolate the button, but small enough relative to effective stacks to preserve postflop maneuvering. Plan: continue aggressively on favorable flops; if the pot becomes multiway I’ll tighten continuation frequency. If Elon 4-bets very large (near an all-in or a heavy polarizing 4-bet), I will fold — against a smaller 4-bet I can consider calling depending on sizing and reads.

I am in the Big Blind with Jc 7c. Elon opened to 200 from the Button, and Minni 3-bet to 900 from the Small Blind. Although J7 suited has some playability, calling an 800-chip raise (8 big blinds) in this spot is not profitable. I would be out of position for the rest of the hand against two opponents, one of whom has shown significant aggression. Given the stack sizes and the strength indicated by a large 3-bet from the Small Blind, the most prudent move is to fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Facing a 4.5x 3bet to 900 from SB Minni after my button min-raise to 200, with 84o (4c8s). This is a weak offsuit hand with poor equity (~20-30% vs typical SB 3bet range of JJ+, AK, AQs+), low playability, and no blockers. Pot 1200, calling 700 commits ~9% of my 79BB stack into a pot where I'll have position postflop but struggle to realize equity on most boards. 4betting as a bluff is suboptimal with no fold equity against deep-stacked Minni's likely calling range and this hand not fitting 4bet bluff profile. Folding the marginal open is standard discipline to avoid difficult spots OOP/IP with trash. Preserve stack for better spots.

Three-handed on the button I should be opening a wide range to steal the blinds and take advantage of position. Tc8d is marginal but has decent playability postflop (top-pair, straight/connector potential) and I don’t want to limp and concede initiative. A standard 3xBB open (300 total) applies pressure to the SB/BB, gives good fold equity, and keeps my range balanced while preserving a deep stack for postflop play if called.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Six offsuit. This is a very weak hand to play out of position against a 3x raise from the Button (Minni), who has been quite active. Calling here would put me in a difficult spot post-flop with a marginal hand and poor position. I will fold to preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

89o is a speculative hand with good playability and straight potential. Against Minni's button raise, her range is wide given her aggressive history (frequent opens from various positions). Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into 450 gives ~2.25:1 (need ~31% equity), and 89o has ~38-40% equity vs. a typical button open (40-50% hands). OOP postflop is a concern but realizable equity justifies calling over folding. No raise as it's not a great 3bet bluff candidate without strong blockers. Defending wide in BB vs. button to maximize stack long-term.

Flop board Qc Qh Ah is coordinated with a paired Queen, Ace-high, and nut flush draw (hearts). My 8c9h has no pair, no straight draw (89 doesn't connect to AQQ), backdoor straight/flush at best, and insignificant equity (~10-20% vs. Minni's wide button range post-flop after my BB defense). OOP first to act with air and poor playability; betting is a poor bluff spot as Minni's range hits this board well (Ax, Qx, flush draws) and she can easily continue. Check-fold exploits her likely aggression (history shows frequent continuation bets), realizes fold equity pre/post, and avoids bloating pot OOP with nothing. Long-term +EV in tournament as stack is deep (76bb).

I completely missed this flop. With Tc8d on Qc Qh Ah I have no pair, no meaningful backdoor draws, and very little showdown value. Elon’s flatting range from the big blind will contain a lot of Ax, Qx and medium/strong broadways and pairs — many hands that beat me or are unwilling to fold on this texture. C-betting here would be a thin bluff into a range that calls or raises too often; with deep stacks and position I prefer pot control. I’ll check and be prepared to fold to any meaningful bet; if he checks back I can take a free turn to reevaluate.

89o remains a complete airball on this A-Q-Q-3 board with no draws, pair, or improvement. Minni's flop check-behind suggests she may have missed the coordinated AQQ flop (e.g., weak Ax, low pairs, or bluffs) rather than trapping nuts, but her button raising range still caps below QQ+/AK often. OOP first to act on turn blank (3s), betting as a bluff is suboptimal: poor blockers (no A/Q), board favors her value range, and she can easily call/raise with medium strength or floats. Checking preserves pot control, realizes equity by seeing free river (minimal reverse implied odds loss), and exploits her aggression history—if she bets turn/river, easy fold. Consistent with flop check-fold plan, +EV long-term in deep-stack tournament by avoiding spew.

The turn (Qc Qh Ah 3s) doesn’t help Tc8d — I have no pair, no meaningful backdoor draw, and very little showdown value. Elon flat-called my open preflop and then checked the flop and turn; his continuing range will be heavy in Qx/Ax and better broadways/pocket pairs that are happy to call or raise. C-betting here would be a thin bluff into a calling-heavy range and would bloat a pot where I’m usually behind. With deep stacks I should control the pot and conserve chips: I’ll check and be prepared to fold to any meaningful bet. If he checks through to the river I’ll re-evaluate and only continue if I pair or face a very weak line.

89o is pure air on this Q-Q-A-3-K river board, missing all draws, pairs, or straights. Minni's button raise range is wide, but her double check-behind on flop/turn (coordinated AQQ then blank 3s) polarizes to either nuts (QQ+/AK/AQ unlikely to check twice OOP), medium showdown value (weak Ax, low pairs, Kx), or bluffs/misses. OOP first to act facing a pot of 650 (76bb deep), betting as a bluff is -EV: no blockers to key value (A/Q/K), board hits her range hard, reverse implied odds if called, and she can hero-call/raise profitably with her stack (219bb). Checking exploits her check-back tendency (history shows passive play postflop in spots like H2, H8, etc.), allows free showdown if she has worse nothing/medium hands, and easy fold to any bet (consistent with flop/turn check-fold plan). Maximizes EV long-term by avoiding unnecessary risk in tournament with 123 hands left.

I have Tc8d on Qc Qh Ah 3s Ks — I missed completely: no pair, no backdoor, almost no showdown value. Elon cold‑called my preflop open and then played very passively (checked flop, turn and river), which is consistent with a range full of Qx/Ax/pocket pairs and medium hands that will happily call a river bet. A bluff here is very thin: the pot is only 650 and many hands in his range beat me or will call, so betting risks chips for little fold equity. I’ll preserve my stack and keep pot control — check and fold to any meaningful bet.

I am on the button with Ace-Five suited, which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is healthy at nearly 100 big blinds. Raising here is standard to take control of the pot and exert pressure on the blinds. A standard 3x raise to 300 is appropriate.

4d9s is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability, especially out of position in the small blind facing a button raise from Flash. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 (calling 250 into 450), requiring too much equity for this trash hand OOP. Flash's button range is wide but still ahead of my holding. Minni may squeeze or defend BB. Best to fold and wait for better spots to preserve stack in tournament.

Facing a button raise to 300 as the Big Blind with 9♦2♠. Pot is 450 and I need to put in 200 more — calling to a total of 300 gives ~3.25:1 pot odds and lets me defend a very wide BB range in a 3-handed spot. 9-2o is too weak to 3-bet as a bluff (no useful blockers, poor postflop playability OOP) and folding is too tight given the price and deep stacks. Plan: call, see the flop, play straightforwardly (check/fold most misses, extract value when I connect).

I called preflop intending to play straightforwardly (check/fold most misses). On 4♠ J♣ 4♣ my 9♦2♠ has no pair, no real backdoor draw and blocks nothing important. The board is paired and relatively dry; a bluff c-bet is low EV here because the raiser’s range hits this texture often and can profitably call or raise. With deep stacks it's better to control the pot — check and fold to meaningful aggression, or only continue to an extremely small probe. If Flash checks back I realize my tiny showdown equity for free.

I am on the button with Ace-Five of clubs. The flop is Jack of clubs, Four of clubs, Four of spades. This is a very strong flop for my hand; I have Nut Flush Draw (backdoor straight potential as well) and one overcard (Ace). Minni, in the Big Blind, checked to me after calling pre-flop. I should place a continuation bet here to represent a Jack or a Four, or simply to build the pot while I have significant equity with my flush draw. A bet of around half the pot is standard here.

I checked the paired flop (4♠ J♣ 4♣) with 9♦2♠ and now face a 325 bet into a 975 pot. I have no pair, no realistic draws (one spade in hand + one on board cannot make a flush by river), and I block nothing meaningful. Calling 325 would require ~20% equity to break even (~1,300 pot after call vs 325 to call); my hand has essentially no showdown equity vs a preflop raiser’s c-bet range (Jx, broadways, pocket pairs). A bluff-raise here is a high-variance move with poor blockers and is likely to get punished. Stick to the preflop plan to check/fold most misses — fold and preserve my deep stack for better spots.

Preflop on the button with A2o (offsuit ace-deuce). Facing presumed SB limp (Minni) and BB check (Flash), pot 150, to call 100. Excellent position with deep stacks (75BB effective). A2o blocks strong Ax hands, has decent equity vs wide limping/checking ranges (~45-50% vs random), and postflop playability in position. Minni appears aggressive but limps from SB are wide and weak. Raising isolates SB, folds out BB often, or builds pot OOP for opponent while retaining fold equity. Standard play: raise to 3x BB (300 total) to steal or play profitably.

Button (Elon) opened to 300. I’m in the small blind with T♠8♣ — a marginal but playable hand in a 3-handed game. Pot is 450 and I need 250 more to call (300 total committed). Calling gives good pot odds (~36% equity required) and keeps the pot manageable while preserving my deep stack (22,275). A small 3-bet to 500 with this hand would be too thin — it’s a bluff-heavy line that invites squeezes and costly decisions out of position. Folding is too tight given the price and hand playability. I’ll call to see a flop and play postflop based on texture and opponents’ reactions.

I am in the big blind with 8d 5d. Elon raised to 300 from the button, and Minni called from the small blind. I need to call 200 more to see the flop in a pot that will be 900. These are excellent pot odds (3.5 to 1), and 85s is a reasonable speculative hand that plays well multi-way. Folding would be too tight given the price, and raising into two opponents, including a massive stack in Minni, is unnecessary. I will call to close the pre-flop action.

I have T♠8♣ on a K♣ 9♦ 3♠ flop — no pair, only a one‑card backdoor spade and otherwise very little. This is a three‑way pot with the button (Elon) as the preflop raiser and Flash in the BB; the board strongly favors the raiser’s range and is fairly dry, so a bluff from me OOP is unlikely to be profitable. I’m first to act and want to control the pot with essentially no showdown value. I’ll check to see how the raiser and BB proceed. Plan: fold to significant aggression (raise/large c‑bet), call small c‑bets (~1/3 pot) occasionally to realize equity and look for a free turn, and only bluff the turn if a very favorable card/spade arrives or both opponents show weakness.

I have 8-high with very limited potential (only backdoor straight and flush draws) on a King-high board. Since I am in a multi-way pot against the pre-flop raiser and a very large stack (Minni), there is no merit in betting into them with air. I will check and likely fold to any significant bet.

Preflop I raised A2o from button after presumed SB limp/call and BB check, standard isolation raise for position and playability. Flop 3s Kc 9d is fairly dry: king-high with no flush draw really popping (spades but only one), possible gutshots but low. Both opponents check, representing weakness. As preflop raiser in position with deep stacks, continuation bet is standard to charge draws, take it down vs missed hands, or get value from weaker kings/9s. Aces block top of range (AK/AQ). Ace-high has showdown value and blocks strong Ax. Bet 1/2 pot (450) for fold equity and pot control, avoiding overbetting marginal strength. Minni deep but aggressive; Flash checked BB.

I called preflop and checked this flop with T♠8♣. Elon (the preflop raiser) fires a small c-bet to 450 (~1/3 pot). I have no pair and only a backdoor spade, but the bet is small so I’d rather see a turn and keep the pot manageable OOP than give up for cheap. Calling here matches my plan to call small c-bets to realize equity and probe for further action; raising is a thin, high-variance bluff from out of position with poor blockers. I’ll call the 450 (I’ve already committed 300 preflop) — total committed becomes 750 — but will fold to a significant raise.

I have 8-high with no significant draws on a King-high board (3s Kc 9d). The pre-flop raiser (Elon) has bet 450 into two opponents, and the small blind (Minni) has already called. With no pair and only a very weak backdoor diamond draw, I have almost zero equity in this pot. Calling 450 would be a waste of chips, and raising as a bluff into two players who have already committed chips to this board is extremely high-risk and low-reward. Folding is the only sensible move here.

I have T♠8♣ on 3♠–K♣–9♦–2♥. I have no pair and essentially no realistic draw (the turn removes the already-minimal backdoor possibilities), so this hand has very little showdown value. Pot is 1,800 and I'm out of position against the preflop raiser who has shown willingness to c‑bet. Betting here would be a thin bluff with poor blockers and risks a raise/shove from hands that beat me (Kx, 9x, broadways) — not worth the stack risk. I’ll check to control the pot and see the river for free; if Elon bets the turn I will release the hand (no equity to continue), and if he checks I get a free card to reassess on the river.

Turn board brings the 2h, pairing my deuce for bottom pair with Ace kicker on a relatively dry king-high board (3s Kc 9d 2h). No flush completed, minimal new draws (possible gutshots like 4-5 for wheel but unlikely). Minni check-called flop c-bet OOP as SB defender, suggesting medium strength like weak Kx, 9x, Ax, or draws, but her turn check indicates no strong hand or reluctance to lead. My hand has some showdown value but is vulnerable to any K, 9, or better pairs. With position and deep stacks (my 68BB effective vs Minni's 200+BB), checking behind achieves pot control, realizes equity against her likely Ax/overs or missed draws for free, and avoids bloating pot with marginal strength. Betting risks folding out worse hands while getting called by better; thin value not worth it given board texture and her history of aggression but passivity here. Free river card is fine as board is unlikely to change drastically.

I hold T♠8♣ on a 3♠–K♣–9♦–2♥–5♥ board — I missed completely and have essentially zero showdown or bluffing equity. Elon (the preflop raiser) c‑bet the flop small and then checked the turn, which is consistent with a wide but often stronger range (Kx, 9x, broadways, or medium pairs). The pot is 1,800 and my bet would be a very thin bluff with poor blockers; Elon can comfortably call a river attempt with many hands that beat me, so my fold equity is low. With a large portion of my stack still live and no reason to create a big pot OOP with air, I preserve chips and go to showdown by checking. If Elon now leads out I will fold to meaningful pressure.

River board completes 3s Kc 9d 2h 5h. I hold A2s (wait, As2c), pairing the deuce on turn for bottom pair top kicker (Ace). Minni check-called flop c-bet as SB defender on king-high board, indicating medium strength like weak Kx, 9x, Ax, or draws, but her turn and river checks suggest weakness or trapping, no monster. Board has possible wheel straight with A4 (unlikely in her range), but no flush. Pot 1800, deep stacks. Bottom pair has showdown value against her likely Ax/overs or missed draws, but thin value bet risks folding out worse while getting snapped by K/9/sets. As preflop raiser in position, checking behind controls pot, realizes equity for free, and avoids difficult river spots. Her passivity post-flop favors showdown over risky bet.

Button with Tc7d in a 3-handed game: marginal but worth opening because of the positional advantage and deep stacks. I want to seize the initiative, get fold equity, and avoid seeing a cheap multiway flop that negates my position. The min-raise to 200 is fine but 3x the BB (300) is a standard open-size that discourages callers and isolates the blinds. Flash (SB) has folded a lot; Elon (BB) can 3-bet but I can play postflop in position or fold to a large 3-bet. Raise to 300 to take the pot initiative and play the hand in position.
T6o is a weak hand to play from the small blind against a button raise. Out of position and with another player still to act, this is a clear fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

AJo offsuit in the big blind facing a button min-raise to 300 (3x). Minni on the button has been aggressive throughout the session, frequently opening wide from late position. AJo has excellent equity against a typical button opening range (dominates most Ax, KQ, suited connectors), around 60-65% equity in most sims. Out of position postflop is a concern, but deep stacks (85BB effective vs Minni's 212BB) allow for postflop play if called. Folding is too tight for such a strong hand; flat-calling risks playing OOP with marginal top pairs or dominated. Standard play is to 3-bet for value, isolation, and fold equity. Sizing to 900 (3x the raise) is balanced, denying equity to worse hands while keeping pot manageable if called. Min raise is to 500, but pot-building size is better.

I opened to 300 on the button with Tc7d and Elon (BB) 3‑bet to 900. To call I must put another 600 into a ~1250 pot, which requires roughly 32% equity to be profitable. Tc7o is frequently dominated by the kinds of hands a competent BB will 3‑bet with (broadways, pairs, A‑x) and doesn’t have the Ace/King blockers that make a good 4‑bet bluff candidate. A min‑4‑bet to 1500 would commit a lot with a poor bluff hand; calling leaves me in tough postflop spots with insufficient immediate pot odds. With a deep stack and many better spots ahead, folding and preserving chips is the highest EV line here.

K9o is a strong hand to open from the button in a 3-handed game. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and exert pressure on the blinds, allowing me to potentially win the hand pre-flop or play in position with a solid hand.

K3o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability, especially out of position in the small blind facing a button raise. Button ranges are wide, but K3o is often dominated by Kx hands and has bad post-flop prospects OOP. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 (35% equity needed), but equity realization is low. No strong blockers for 3bet bluff. Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid marginal spots. Opponents' histories show aggression, but doesn't change fundamentals here.

Facing a button open to 300 from my big blind with 6♥8♥. Pot is 450; I need 200 more to call (300 total committed). 6-8 suited plays well heads-up — decent equity and strong implied odds against a likely wide button opening range. Calling costs 200 into 450 (I’d need ~30.8% equity) which this hand can achieve; stacks are deep (my 21,175 vs his 9,450), so implied odds are good. I’d rather defend and see a flop than 3-bet small and build a big OOP pot with a marginal hand or fold and concede the blind. Call to realize equity and play post-flop.

I have 6♥8♥ on 2♦7♥5♥ — a very strong combo draw: an open-ended straight (4 or 9) plus a four‑card heart draw. That’s ~15 unique outs (~32% to hit on the turn, ~54% by the river). Out of position I want to semi-bluff to build the pot when I hit and to fold out a lot of single‑pair or ace‑high hands. A ~half‑pot bet preserves fold equity without overcommitting; if called I’ll reevaluate on the turn and press when I improve or pot‑control on blanks. Betting 325 (≈1/2 pot).

On a low, wet board of 2d-7h-5h, Minni has led out with a donk bet of half the pot. While I opened pre-flop, my hand (K9o) has completely missed this board, and I have no significant draws or backdoor equity other than two overcards. In a three-handed game, Minni's range for calling from the big blind and leading on this texture includes many small pairs and straight/flush draws that are currently ahead of me or have significant equity. Calling here would be a speculative float with no real plan for the turn, and raising would be turning a hand with some showdown value into an expensive bluff against a range that likely connected with the low cards. Folding is the most disciplined way to preserve my stack.

3-handed on the button with ATo offsuit, a strong hand with great position and postflop playability. Pot is 150, facing a 100 bet (likely a small raise or limp-bet), offering decent pot odds but we want to take control, build the pot when ahead, and apply pressure. Standard raise sizing to ~3x the bet (300 total) to isolate or steal blinds/continuation. Equity favorable against likely calling ranges (dominated aces, suited connectors, pairs), and fold equity high given history of folds to aggression. Minni deep and aggressive but Flash shorter; raising maintains initiative for stack growth.

3♣9♦ is a very weak, awkward hand — especially out of position. Elon opened to 300 (standard raise) and Flash (BB) is a sticky caller; calling 250 more would give me a multiway pot OOP with little equity and many difficult decisions postflop. A tiny re-raise to 500 would be a light, non-credible bluff and unnecessarily escalates the pot against two deeper stacks. Preserve chips and wait for a better spot — fold.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Nine of Hearts, a very strong suited connector in a three-handed game. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button. My pot odds are approximately 2.25 to 1 (200 to call into a 450 pot), which is more than enough to justify a call with this hand's post-flop playability. While a 3-bet is a viable aggressive option, calling allows me to keep the pot smaller out of position and potentially outplay him on favorable flops. Elon has shown a willingness to fold to 3-bets in previous hands, but T9s is a hand I prefer to play post-flop rather than using as a pure bluffing candidate here.

I missed the flop completely. The King-high board favors Elon's pre-flop raising range from the button. Out of position, the standard play is to check to the aggressor. I have no immediate draws or pairs, so I am prepared to fold to most bets unless the turn brings significant improvement (like a heart or a straight draw).

Preflop raiser on a dry K-6-4 rainbow flop that favors my button opening range (frequent kings, Ax, broadways) over Flash's BB calling range (capped at medium pairs, suited connectors that rarely hit). AdTs is ace-high with good blockers and decent showdown value. Flash checks, signaling weakness; standard continuation bet to deny equity, build pot vs worse Ax/draws, and apply fold equity. History shows Flash folds to aggression postflop often (e.g., Hand 21, 28). 1/2 pot sizing (325) balances value and protection, maintaining initiative in position for stack growth in tournament.

I have missed the flop completely with Ten-Nine of hearts. The board of King-Six-Four rainbow heavily favors the pre-flop aggressor's range, as Elon is likely to have many Kings, pocket pairs, or at the very least, better high-card value. I have no direct draws to a straight or a flush. Attempting a check-raise bluff here against a standard continuation bet is risky and likely unprofitable given the board texture and Elon's position. The most disciplined play is to fold and wait for a more favorable situation.

3-handed on the button with Kc4c (suited king). This is a marginal but playable hand in position — it has backdoor flush/straight potential and plays well postflop from the button. Limping is too passive against two opponents who have shown aggression; opening lets me use position and fold equity to steal the blinds. I'll make a standard 3x BB open to 300 total to apply pressure but keep the raise realistic to avoid committing too much with a dominated hand. If I'm called, I can play postflop with position and c-bet favorable textures; if I face a large 3-bet (especially >~3x my raise), I will fold K4s as it's often dominated.
96 offsuit is a weak hand to play from the small blind against a button raise. I am out of position and the hand has poor playability. I will fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

2h9s is a trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. OOP in BB facing a button raise from an aggressive opener like Minni, who frequently raises wide. Pot odds are decent (200 to win 450, ~3:1), but equity against her range is poor (~25-30% at best), and playability post-flop is awful. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots in a long tournament.

I am on the button with 8s 6h. In a 3-handed game, opening the button is standard, but 86o is at the lower end of the opening range. Given Minni's large stack and history of aggressive play and 3-betting, I prefer to fold this hand and wait for a better spot rather than risking a 3-bet or playing a weak hand out of position post-flop if called.

Preflop in the small blind heads-up vs BB after button fold. K6o is a marginal hand but has decent equity (~55-60% vs a typical loose BB defending range), a strong king blocker reducing villain's Kx, and top pair potential. Folding SB OOP is too tight in an unopened pot; we want to realize equity cheaply. Raising is possible as a steal but K6o plays poorly postflop OOP if called. Limping/completing balances our SB range, induces BB checks, and keeps pot small to play profitably postflop despite position disadvantage. Minni's history shows aggression but with deep stacks (91BB effective), pot control is key early. Goal is long-term stack growth, not forcing action with mediocre hand.

Elon (SB) just limped/called 50 which looks like a weak, wide range. Heads-up 7♥9♣ is playable and has good equity against a limp — I want to take the initiative and isolate. I’ll raise to 300 total (≈3x BB) to apply pressure and deny free flops; if he folds I scoop the pot, if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously postflop and fold to significant 3-bet aggression with this marginal holding. With my deep stack I can exploit his passive limp but I won’t bloat the pot with a mediocre hand.

Preflop SB vs BB heads-up after BTN fold. Already limped with K6o, which has good equity (~45-55% vs typical BB raise-over-limp range including broadways, suited, pairs), strong K blocker, and postflop potential (TPTK, straights). Pot odds excellent: calling 200 into 400 (pot becomes 600, ~3:1 implied). Folding too weak after entering voluntarily; wastes equity and limping range. 3-betting risky OOP with marginal hand vs aggressive Minni (history of large bets/raises, e.g., Hand 6 shove), could face 4-bet or tough spots postflop. Calling keeps pot small, realizes equity cheaply, balances range (mix of calls/raises post-limp), controls SPR (~15:1 effective deep). Long-term stack growth prioritizes playable spots over folding equity here.

Heads-up pot OOP on the flop with Ks6d on 5s7s2s board. My Ks gives me the nut flush draw (no spade higher than K on board), one of the best possible draws here (~35-45% equity baseline vs villain's BB raise-over-limp range of pairs, suited connectors, broadways, Ax suited). Board is highly coordinated and scary: monotone spades, potential straights (34,56,48, etc.), opens draws. Villain's range hits this board decently (spade flush draws, sets, two-pair combos like 75s,52s rare), but much weaker value and air that can bet/fold. Preflop limp-call was small ball with marginal K6o, but flop transformed it into a monster semi-bluff spot. Deep stacks (91BB eff ~14BB SPR now). Betting risks folding out better equity realization and faces check-raise risk OOP; standard play is check to induce bluffs/value bets from medium pairs/draws (e.g., 88-JJ, lower FD), then raise (pot or shove depending on sizing). Balances my limping range (check calls pre with draws now check-raising), exploits Minni's aggression (frequent postflop betting/raising in history, e.g., Hands 6,7,16). Check keeps pot controllable if checked behind (turn equity still strong), maximizes fold equity on raise. Long-term EV prioritizes check-raise line over donk bet OOP.

I have top pair (7s) with a weak 9 kicker on a monotone spade flop (5♠-7♠-2♠). Elon limped/called preflop, so his range is wide and includes many unpaired hands as well as some spades. I want to extract value from unpaired overcards and deny equity to backdoor draws, but I don’t want to bloat the pot with a vulnerable top pair that can be crushed by any spade. Per my preflop plan, proceed cautiously postflop and fold to significant aggression. A small c-bet (~1/3 pot) pressures air and charges draws while keeping my commitment low if he check-raises. If he flats, I’ll re-evaluate on the turn; if he raises strongly I’ll fold.

Facing a small 200 bet into an 800 pot (~25% pot) on a highly draw-heavy monotone spade board (5s7s2s) OOP with Ks6d, giving me the nut flush draw (~35% equity standalone, higher vs villain's range). Preflop limp-call was small-ball with K6o, but flop hit massive equity. Villain (Minni in BB) raised over my SB limp pre (aggressive history: frequent postflop bets/raises in pots like Hands 6,7,16,31), but this tiny bet sizing indicates weakness: polarized thin C-bet with air (overcards, backdoors), weak pairs (88-JJ unlikely to bet small), inferior draws (non-nut FD like JsXs, 8sXs, straight draws 34/48/56 suited spade-heavy), or monsters trapping (sets rare, two-pair 75s/52s/65s possible but small bet doesn't fit value). Raising exploits this: extracts value from trapped medium strength/worse draws/pairs, fold equity vs bluffs/A-high/overcards (key vs BB wide defend/raise range post-limp), denies equity realization. Calling OOP risks turn barrel/blank check-raise spots or free river cards shrinking equity; shoving too deep/overplays draw. Pot raise to 800 (4x their bet, SPR post ~5:1 my stack) balances semi-bluff/value, induces folds from ~50% of range while stacking off vs worse. Fits long-term stack growth: realize +EV equity aggressively in deep 88BB+ spot vs known aggressor who folds to resistance (e.g., Hand 21 fold postflop). Blocks Ks denies nut advantage to villain.

I c-bet small (200 ≈1/3 pot) to charge draws and get value from worse, per plan. Elon’s check‑raise to 800 is a large, polarized action on a monotone spade flop (5♠‑7♠‑2♠). To have a made flush on this flop he only needs two spades in hand, and there are many two‑spade combos in a limp/call range — plus sets (55/22/77) are plausible. My 7♥9♣ is top pair but has no spade and a weak kicker, so it fares very poorly versus those ranges. Pot is 1,600 and it’s 600 to call (≈27% pot odds), but my equity vs the realistic check‑raise range is well below that and calling will put me in very tough, commitment decisions on later streets. I’ll preserve a big stack and fold this marginal one‑pair hand to the strong flop aggression.

3-handed on the button in an unopened pot (just blinds posted). Q5o is a standard raising hand from this position due to excellent post-flop position and wide button opening ranges (around 50-70% of hands in 3-handed play). Opponents have shown loose tendencies with frequent check/calls and defenses, but my position allows me to realize equity well. Raising steals blinds often or builds pot IP with some showdown value from the Queen. Standard size of 3x BB (300 total). Folding too tight here; limping poor.

Elon opened to 300 (pot 450, I need 250). I have A♣4♣ in the small blind with a big stack (21,175) versus two ~9k stacks. A4s is worth defending/playing aggressively in 3-handed play: it has blocker value and good postflop playability. Folding is too tight; just calling leaves me out of position and likely in a multiway pot against two active players. I want to isolate the button and take the initiative, so I 3-bet to apply pressure. A raise to 1,200 total (~4x the open) gives solid fold equity (often folding out the big blind) while not overcommitting if I face a large 4-bet — in that case I can fold rather than get stacked with a marginal kicker. If called, I’ll use position/initiative to continue aggressively on favorable flops. Action: raise to 1,200.

I am holding Ten-Deuce offsuit in the Big Blind. The Button opened to 3 big blinds and the Small Blind 3-bet to 12 big blinds. My hand is extremely weak and has zero playability against a raise and a 3-bet. There is no reason to commit any chips to this pot. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Facing a 4x 3-bet to 1200 from the aggressive SB after my standard button open-raise with Q5o. Q5o is a marginal opening hand but poor in 3-bet pots: low equity (~25-30% vs typical SB 3-bet range of pairs, broadways, Ax), poor post-flop playability (dominated Qx, few draws, hard to realize equity even IP). Pot odds require ~36% equity (900 to call into 2500 pot), which we don't have. Raising as a bluff is spewy with weak blockers and commits too much of stack (min-raise to 2100 uses ~22% stack). Minni's history shows frequent aggression but profitable to fold marginals here deep-stacked in tournament; preserve stack for better spots. Standard fold.

I'm on the button with Qh2c — a marginal offsuit holding, but in a 3-handed game position is very powerful and I should be opening a wide stealing range. The pot is 150 and it costs 100 to just call; a limp here is passive and invites squeezes. I choose to raise to 3x the big blind (300 total) to take the initiative, apply pressure to the blinds, and keep the pot manageable relative to my deep stack (21,625). Min-raising to 200 is too small and will encourage more defense; 300 is large enough to discourage many calls but not commit me. If I face a substantial 3-bet I'll fold Q2o; if called I'll c-bet favorable flops and play cautiously on ace/queen-high or heavily connected boards.

In a 3-handed game, Ace-Seven suited is a strong hand, particularly against a button open. Minni has been active and aggressive, so 3-betting (raising) here allows me to take the initiative, potentially win the pot immediately, and define Minni's range. It also discourages Elon in the Big Blind from entering the pot with a wide range. I will raise to 1000 to apply pressure and capitalize on the strength of my hand.

86o is a very weak hand with poor equity against a strong 3-bet range from Flash in the SB after Minni's button open-raise. Facing a 3-bet to 1000 (10BB), the pot odds require ~39% equity to call 900 into 1400, but 86o likely has 20-30% or less vs. QQ+, AKo/s, etc. No strong blockers or playability postflop. Minni may continue, making multiway pot worse. Standard fold to preserve stack in tournament.

I opened wide from the button with Qh2c, but Flash’s sizable 3‑bet to 1,000 (I must call 700 into a 1,400 pot; min‑raise to 1,700) puts me in a poor spot. Calling requires ~33% equity to break even, and Q2o is heavily dominated by a realistic 3‑bet range (Ax, Kx, broadways, pairs) — far less equity than needed. A small 4‑bet bluff here is non‑credible given deep stacks and would commit me to difficult post‑flop decisions if called. I’ll preserve my deep stack and stick to my pre‑flop plan: fold to a substantial 3‑bet with this holding.

I am on the button with Ac 9s, which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. Standard strategy dictates opening for a raise here to apply pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot. A 3x open is standard and effective.

Hand is 3s6h, trash offsuit with poor playability and no high card value. Facing a button raise from Flash, who likely has a wide range, but from SB OOP, calling or raising is -EV. Pot odds are ~1.8:1 but equity realization post-flop is poor. Minni in BB may isolate or check. Preserve stack for better spots in 3-handed play.

Facing a button open to 300 from Flash, I'm in the big blind with 2♠9♦ — a very weak, unsuited hand. Calling costs me 200 more (total 300) into a 450 pot, which is a cheap price to see a flop (break-even equity ≈ 31%). I'm out of position, so 3-betting light OOP is suboptimal: little fold equity and likely difficult post‑flop decisions if called. Flash's open sizing looks standard and their stack (~9100) means a light 3‑bet won't necessarily take it down. I will call to 300, see the flop, and proceed cautiously — folding to heavy aggression unless I pair or pick up a legitimate draw.

Flop: Kc 6c 4s; my hand 2♠9♦ — no pair, no club, no real draw. I called preflop as a cheap speculative play, but on this king‑high texture out of position I have very little equity and poor playability. There's no cost to checking; best line is pot control and folding to meaningful aggression. I will check and re‑evaluate on the turn only if I pair or pick up a legitimate draw — otherwise I fold to a standard continuation bet.

I am the pre-flop aggressor and the board has come out King-high, which significantly favors my perceived range over the big blind's defending range. Minni has checked, which is the standard response from the defender. I will place a continuation bet of approximately half the pot to maintain pressure and represent a King, which should force folds from many of Minni's non-pairing hands and small pocket pairs. I also have Ace-high and a backdoor club flush draw as potential insurance.

I will fold. I called preflop as a cheap speculative play, but on this K‑high flop (Kc 6c 4s) with 2♠9♦ I have no pair, no club, and no realistic draw. Flash’s bet of 300 into a 950 pot means a call requires ~300/(950+300)=24% equity — far more than my hand has. I’m out of position, facing a standard continuation bet that likely represents a king or a club draw; check‑raising a bluff OOP is unnecessarily risky here. Preserve the stack and wait for a better spot.

3-handed on the button, first to act pre-flop with K6o. This is a profitable stealing spot with fold equity against blinds. K6o blocks kings and has decent playability in position if called. Standard RFI range from BTN 3-handed includes K6o. Raising to 2.5x BB (250) is a good size to balance value and bluffs while denying equity to calling ranges. Limping is suboptimal as it kills fold equity and plays OOP post-flop.

Facing a button open to 250 from my SB with 2♣3♠ (pot 400, 200 to call). This is a weak, offsuit wheel hand with almost no playability out of position, and the big blind is yet to act — I can easily face a 3-bet or a multiway pot where I’m disadvantaged. With deep stacks I want to conserve chips for better spots and avoid OOP postflop speculation with trash. Fold.

In a 3-handed game, the Button's opening range is typically very wide, often exceeding 50% of hands. Given Elon's raise to 250, I am being offered pot odds of 2.67 to 1 (150 to call into a 400 pot). 8-6 offsuit is a standard defending hand in the Big Blind against a 2.5x open in this configuration; folding would be overly tight and permit the Button to profitably open any two cards. I will call the 150-chip difference to see a flop and navigate post-flop based on the board texture and Elon's betting patterns.

On a board of Jd-6c-7c, I have flopped middle pair (sixes) with an 8 kicker. While the board is quite coordinated with both flush and straight possibilities, checking to the pre-flop aggressor is the standard and most prudent play out of position. Middle pair is a solid bluff-catcher and has some potential to improve to a straight if a 5 or 9 falls. By checking, I can gauge Elon's aggression; if he bets, I plan to call to see a turn, as my hand is too strong to fold to a single continuation bet on this texture.

Heads-up on the flop with bottom pair and king kicker (6h Kc on Jd 6c 7c board). This gives me decent showdown value and blocks some king-high flush draws. Flash defended wide from the BB vs my BTN open, so his range includes draws (clubs, 58/89 OESD), weaker pairs, and floats. The board is draw-heavy with flush and straight draws, so betting protects my hand, charges equity, and denies free cards. Position allows me to c-bet profitably as part of a balanced range that includes Jx, sets, and two-pair. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws that can improve, and pot control is less important than realizing equity here. Sizing to ~2/3 pot (350) builds the pot while leaving room for turns/rivers and maintaining fold equity against marginal hands.

I have flopped middle pair (sixes) with an 8 kicker on a wet board of Jd-6c-7c. Elon, opening from the button, has a wide range and is expected to continuation bet here with a high frequency, including many draws (flush draws, straight draws like T9 or 98) and air. Against a 350 bet into a 550 pot, I am getting roughly 2.6:1 on a call. Folding middle pair to a single bet on this texture would be exploitable and too tight. Raising doesn't accomplish much as it forces out his bluffs and keeps only his better hands in. Calling allows me to keep his range wide, see a turn, and potentially improve to two pair or trips, or simply win if the hand goes to showdown and he was bluffing. I will call the 350.

The 3d on the turn is a relatively neutral card that doesn't change the texture of the board significantly. I still hold middle pair (sixes) with an 8 kicker, which functions well as a bluff-catcher against the Button's wide range. Following the standard line after calling on the flop, I will check to the aggressor. This allows Elon to continue with his semi-bluffs or check back his marginal hands. Leading here would likely only be called by better hands and would force out the bluffs I'm looking to catch.

Turn card 3d is a low, relatively blank card that doesn't complete many strong draws from Flash's flop calling range (primarily clubs, OESD like 58/89, weaker pairs, Ax floats). It adds a backdoor diamond draw but doesn't heavily impact ranges. My bottom pair (6h pairing 6c) with Kc kicker (blocking top pair Kx and some club flushes) has solid showdown value against his wide BB defending range vs BTN open. He checked flop and turn, capping somewhat at medium strength or missed draws. Betting thin value here risks raises from Jx+, sets, or 2p, while worse (A-high, gutshots, missed equity) often fold, reducing EV. Checking controls pot (1250, deep stacks), realizes equity safely, and position allows responding optimally on river: call down lighter, raise bluffs, or check back showdown. Consistent with balanced range where I check back some showdown hands post-flop bet. Maximizes EV long-term.

On the river, the board is Jd-6c-7c-3d-5h. My pair of sixes remains third pair. The 5h on the river is a significant card as it completes potential straights for hands like 9-8 (which was an open-ended straight draw on the flop) and any hand containing a 4 (3-4-5-6-7). Elon checked the turn after I checked to him, which suggests he either has a marginal hand (like a weak Jack, a 7, or a 6) or a missed draw. While my sixes have some showdown value, betting here doesn't accomplish much; better hands like a Jack or a straight will call, and worse hands will likely fold. Checking is the most prudent play to reach showdown cheaply or to act as a bluff-catcher if Elon decides to bet with a missed flush draw or high cards. I will check.

River board Jd6c7c3d5h completes no flush and adds straight draw possibilities (e.g., 4x for 3-4-5-6-7 straight). My bottom pair (6h) with Kc kicker has strong showdown value against Flash's wide BB defending range vs BTN open, which includes missed clubs (OESD 89, flush draws), A-high floats, weaker Ax/Jx, and some gutshots. He called flop c-bet (draw-heavy board) then checked turn and river, capping at medium strength or air. Betting risks folding out worse hands (A-high, missed equity) while getting called/raised by Jx+, sets, 67/Two-pair/straights. Checking realizes equity safely, controls pot (1250 into 8625 effective stacks), and leverages position to showdown profitably. Consistent with balanced range: c-bet flop for protection/value, check turn (blankish), check river showdown. Maximizes EV by avoiding unnecessary risk in multiway tournament simulation.

Three-handed on the button with K♦2♠ — a weak kicker but decent enough to open in position versus only the blinds. In 3-max play my RFI should be wide; raising steals the antes/blinds and preserves initiative. I’ll raise to the minimum (200) to apply pressure for a small cost — if either blind 3-bets substantially I’ll fold (K2o is frequently dominated), but if I'm called I keep positional advantage and can c-bet or pot-control depending on the flop. Raising here maximizes expected value versus simply limping or folding.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Three offsuit. The button (Minni) has opened with a min-raise to 200. Although the pot odds are decent, Q3o is a very weak hand that realizes equity poorly out of position. Given Minni's active play and the risk of being squeezed by the Big Blind or playing a multi-way pot with a marginal holding, folding is the most disciplined and standard play here to preserve my stack.

Preflop in BB facing a min-raise to 200 from Minni on the button after SB folds. Hand is 93s, suited connector/gap with flush and straight potential. Pot is 350, calling 100 more for excellent pot odds (paying 22% to win current pot). OOP postflop but deep stacks allow realization of equity if we hit. Minni button raises are wide based on history (frequent small raises), so folding too tight. Raising marginal with this hand risks bloating pot OOP. Standard play is to call and see flop speculatively.

Flop hits me with top pair (nines) but with a terrible kicker (trey), on a coordinated board with two hearts (flush draw) and possible straight draws (e.g., 87, T8, JT). OOP vs button who raised wide preflop and is likely to c-bet frequently. Pot control is key; betting risks bloating pot with a marginal hand vulnerable to domination (Qx, better 9x) or draws raising. Checking allows seeing villain's action: fold equity if check-fold, pot odds to call small c-bet, or check-raise if overcards/draws. Deep stacks favor realizing equity cautiously. Standard OOP play with thin value on drawy board.

I opened preflop from the button with K2o and picked up the initiative. The flop Qh‑9d‑6h is fairly dry heads‑up (two hearts are the only real draw), and my K‑high has decent showdown value vs many of the BB’s wide calling hands. I should continuation‑bet a high frequency here to fold out unpaired holdings (Ax, Jx, Tx, small pairs) and to get value from worse hands while keeping the pot small relative to our deep stacks. A small sizing (~1/3 pot) preserves flexibility — if Elon raises strongly I’ll fold K2o (we’re often dominated), if he calls I can pot‑control or barre­l on favorable turns. Betting 150 into the 450 pot achieves that balance.

Facing a small continuation bet of 150 into a 600 pot from Minni on the button after a wide preflop raising range. I have top pair (nines) with a terrible kicker (3s), suited for backdoor spade flush draw on a coordinated board with flush draw and straight draw possibilities (e.g., 78, T8, JT for straights, heart draws). Pot odds are excellent (~3.2:1 implied), paying just 20% of the pot to realize equity OOP. Calling maintains pot control on this draw-heavy board, keeps villain's bluffs/draws/medium strength hands in, and allows seeing turn cheaply with position disadvantage but deep stacks. Raising risks bloating pot OOP with a vulnerable hand easily dominated by Qx or better 9x, and folding top pair is too tight given bet sizing and history of Minni's wide aggression. Plan to reevaluate turn based on action and board texture.

On the turn Kc, the board is 9d Qh 6h Kc, a highly coordinated draw-heavy texture with completed flush draws (hearts), straight draws (T8, J10, 78, etc.), and the king bringing strong overpair possibilities or Kx top pair. I hold top pair nines with a terrible 3 kicker (93s), backdoor spade flush draw, but no immediate draws or straight potential. OOP first to act after calling a small flop c-bet from Minni's wide button range. Betting thin value risks bloating the pot OOP against better Qx/9x/Kx/draws that can raise or call profitably; fold equity is low given villain's aggression. Checking is optimal for pot control: induces bluffs/misses from villain's wide range, allows cheap realization if check-fold or small bet, and sets up check-raise if overbet. Deep stacks favor seeing river cheaply where I can improve or villain bricks. Folding top pair too tight pre-turn action.

I have K♦2♠ on Kc‑Qh‑9d‑6h‑(turn)Kc: top pair but a very weak kicker. Pot is 750 and Elon checked the turn after calling my flop c‑bet, so his range is likely Qx, 9x, 6x, some pocket pairs and heart draws — many hands that are behind my King or that I can charge. Betting now gains value from worse pairs (Qx/9x/6x/etc.), charges heart draws, and keeps control of the hand rather than giving a free river card. I don’t want to build the pot too large with a marginal kicker, but I do want to deny odds to draws and extract value. A half‑pot bet (375) is a good balance: large enough to fold out some equity-chasing hands and get calls from worse, yet small relative to our stack so a big check‑raise would strongly indicate a Kx/two‑pair and I can fold. If called, I’ll re‑evaluate the river based on the card; if raised large I’ll lean to folding the marginal kicker.

Facing a 375 bet into 1125 pot on the turn (Kc) from Minni's wide button range after small flop c-bet. Board 9d Qh 6h Kc is draw-heavy (hearts, open-enders like T8/JT/78), king hits strong hands (AK/KQ/K9) but also overcards in her range. Top pair nines (9s) with 3 kicker is thin but has showdown value; pot odds excellent (375 to win 1500, ~20% equity needed, and hand crushes bluffs/draws/worse pairs, blocks some 9x). OOP check-call maintains pot control, induces thinner river value from villain while realizing equity cheaply deep-stacked. Raising bloats pot OOP vs potential better (Qx/Kx) or draws that shove; folding top pair too tight vs small sizing and aggressive history (Minni cbets wide, check-folds sometimes). Plan: call, evaluate river (improve to two-pair unlikely but showdown ok).

River 3h pairs my trey, improving me from top pair weak kicker to two pair (9s and 3s) on a board 9d Qh 6h Kc 3h. This beats all weaker pairs, many draws that missed, and bluffs from Minni's wide button raising and c-betting range (small flop/turn bets suggest continuation rather than nuts). However, the board completes the nut flush draw (three hearts), possible flushes (e.g., Ahxh, Khxh in range), top two pair like K9/Q9, or sets/trips. OOP first to act in a 1500 pot deep-stacked vs aggressive Minni who has bet two streets. Betting thin value risks her folding worse (AJ/KT bluffs/draws) or raising better (flush/K9); fold equity low given my calling range looks strong. Checking optimal: realizes equity for free if check-behind (common vs missed draws/overcards), induces bluffs/value from worse one-pair (Qx/Kx), pots control OOP, and allows hero-call if small bet or fold to overbet flush. Her history of wide aggression favors check to trap/exploit. Plan showdown or call stations.

I have K♦2♠ — top pair but a very weak kicker on a river that just completed a three‑heart board (Q♥‑9♦‑6♥‑K♣‑3♥). Elon called my flop and turn bets, so his range is wide but weighted toward Qx/9x/mid pairs and some heart combinations. A small value bet would get called by worse pairs (Qx, 9x, small pockets) but also risks a check‑raise or thin value bet from any two‑heart combos that now make a flush. With deep stacks and only a marginal kicker I don’t want to inflate the pot or face a sizing that puts me to a tough decision — checking preserves showdown value and can induce bluffs. Plan: check and, if Elon fires a small river probe (≈≤1/3 pot) I’ll call for showdown; if he bets big or check‑raises I’ll fold my marginal K‑2.

I am on the button in a 3-handed game. My hand, King-Trey offsuit, is a marginal candidate for an open. Given that Minni in the big blind has a very large stack and has been playing aggressively, and Elon in the small blind is also quite active, I expect to face a lot of resistance. K3o does not play well post-flop, especially in multi-way pots or against 3-bets. I will choose to fold this hand and look for a more advantageous situation.

Preflop in SB vs BB heads-up after BTN fold. 94s is a suited connector with good playability, straight and flush potential. Excellent pot odds: calling 50 more into 150 pot (3.5:1), equity ~35%+ vs typical BB defending range. OOP but manageable postflop. History shows Minni defends wide preflop and often checks/calls passively postflop, allowing value and bluff opportunities. Folding too tight in SB; raising risks unnecessary variance without isolation value. Standard play is to complete.

Heads-up in the big blind with 5♦3♠ — a very weak, unsuited, unconnected hand. Elon (SB) merely completed to the big blind, showing a wide range but nothing that forces me to invest chips preflop. With deep stacks (~20k) and only 200 in the pot, I’ll check and see a cheap flop rather than bluff-raise or commit chips. Postflop plan: fold to a sizeable continuation bet unless I pair the board, make two pair/trips, or pick up a strong draw (open-ender or a backdoor combo). If he checks back I’ll realize equity cheaply and reassess on the turn.

Flop board 2s6c2d is paired and dry, favoring bluffs from SB completing range which includes more 2x suited hands and overcards for turn playability. 94s has backdoor spade flush draw (with 2s on board) and straight potential (e.g., 3-5, 7-8 runners), plus live 9. Minni's BB checking range is wide and passive postflop based on history (frequent check/calls, folds to aggression). OOP first to act with 200 pot, a small c-bet of ~60% pot exploits fold equity against weak holdings like Ax, Kx, low pairs below 6, while building pot with equity.
